title: Mizuho Adjusts Price Target on Alliant Energy to $74 From $73, Maintains Neutral Rating
summary: Mizuho has adjusted its price target for Alliant Energy (NASDAQ: LNT) to $74, up from $73, while maintaining a Neutral rating on the stock. This update reflects a slight increase in the firm's valuation for the utility company. The article also mentions recent analyst activity including RBC initiating coverage with an Outperform rating and UBS adjusting its price target for Alliant Energy.

title: Alliant Energy Corporation $LNT Shares Bought by Aventail Capital Group LP
summary: Aventail Capital Group LP significantly increased its holdings in Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT) by 261.1% in the third quarter, bringing its total to 244,725 shares valued at $16.5 million. This move is part of broader institutional investor activity, with other major funds like Vanguard and Massachusetts Financial Services also adjusting their stakes in the utility company. Alliant Energy recently reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst estimates, and increased its quarterly dividend.

title: Progress 2026: Construction of new Bobcat Energy Center to begin late this summer
summary: Alliant Energy received approval from the Iowa Utilities Commission in December 2025 to build the 720-megawatt Bobcat Energy Center (BEC) in Marshalltown, adjacent to its existing generating station. Construction is set to begin late in the third quarter of 2026, with the facility expected to be operational by 2029. The project is anticipated to bring significant economic benefits to Marshalltown through construction jobs, tax revenue, and increased energy security, reinforcing the area's role in electricity generation.

title: Alliant Energy Corp stock hits all-time high at 72.67 USD
summary: Alliant Energy Corp (LNT) stock has hit an all-time high of $72.67, with a 52-week high of $72.65 and an 18.71% one-year price return. Despite this, InvestingPro analysis suggests the stock may be overvalued. The company has a market capitalization of $18.63 billion, a dividend yield of 2.99%, and has raised its dividend for 22 consecutive years.

title: Alliant Energy keeps climbing while earnings estimates slip
summary: Alliant Energy (LNT) is facing conflicting signals from Wall Street, with some analysts trimming near-term earnings forecasts while others raise price targets and maintain bullish ratings, largely due to Iowa's favorable regulatory framework and tax credit mechanisms. The company's valuation is seen as supported by regulatory stability and data center demand, despite a P/E ratio higher than industry averages. Investors are weighing a slight reduction in Q1 2026 earnings estimates against strong long-term growth prospects and increased dividends.

title: Swiss National Bank Sells 37,000 Shares of Alliant Energy Corporation $LNT
summary: Swiss National Bank reduced its stake in Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT) by 4.8% in the 3rd quarter, selling 37,000 shares and now owning 740,500 shares valued at $49.9 million. Other institutional investors like Vanguard Group Inc. and Massachusetts Financial Services Co. MA increased their holdings. Wall Street analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with an average target price of $75.33 for Alliant Energy.

title: Legal & General Group Plc Has $155.47 Million Position in Alliant Energy Corporation $LNT
summary: Legal & General Group Plc reduced its stake in Alliant Energy Corporation by 2.6% in the third quarter, now holding $155.47 million worth of shares. Other institutional investors also adjusted their positions in Alliant Energy, which recently increased its quarterly dividend and reported strong earnings that beat analyst estimates. The company currently has a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts, with several recent reports issuing "outperform" or "buy" ratings and price target increases.

title: Swiss National Bank Sells 37,000 Shares of Alliant Energy Corporation
summary: The Swiss National Bank reduced its stake in Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT) by 4.8% during the 3rd quarter, selling 37,000 shares to bring its total ownership to 740,500 shares. This divestment by a major institutional investor could indicate a shift in sentiment regarding the utility company's stock, prompting other investors to pay attention. The reasons behind the sale were not explicitly stated.

title: AI cuts AES safety probe time in half for US power workers
summary: AES (NYSE: AES) has deployed Haven Safety AI across its U.S. utility and renewables operations, significantly reducing incident investigation times by over 50%. This AI-native platform enhances root-cause identification and provides better visibility into systemic risks, demonstrating a proactive approach to worker safety. The rapid development and large-scale implementation of Haven Safety AI reflect AES's broader strategy to integrate AI into its operations for increased efficiency and improved safety performance.

title: Magnetar Financial LLC Purchases Shares of 42,140 Public Service Enterprise Group Incorporated $PEG
summary: Magnetar Financial LLC has acquired a new position in Public Service Enterprise Group (NYSE: PEG) during the third quarter, purchasing 42,140 shares valued at approximately $3.52 million. This move is part of a broader trend of institutional investors adjusting their stakes in the utilities provider, with several hedge funds increasing their holdings. Analysts have a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating for PEG, with a target price of $92.73, and the company recently increased its quarterly dividend.

title: Alliant Energy Corporation $LNT Shares Sold by Natixis Advisors LLC
summary: Natixis Advisors LLC recently reduced its stake in Alliant Energy (NASDAQ:LNT) by 5.8%, selling 14,999 shares, though it still holds a significant position. The utility company reported strong quarterly earnings, beating expectations with $0.60 EPS and $1.06 billion in revenue, and subsequently increased its quarterly dividend to $0.535 per share. Alliant Energy maintains a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts, with shares trading around $70.81 and a market capitalization of $18.21 billion.

title: Assessing Evergy (EVRG) Valuation After A Steady Multi‑Year Total Return Run
summary: Evergy (EVRG) has shown a steady performance with a 26.81% one-year and 52.54% three-year total shareholder return. Despite its current price of $81.02 being below the average analyst target of $87.50, suggesting a 6.7% undervaluation with a fair value of $86.86, its P/E ratio of 21.8x is higher than peers and the US Electric Utilities average. Investors are advised to consider both the growth potential from grid modernization and clean energy initiatives, as well as the valuation risk indicated by its P/E ratio.

title: RJM & Company Advises Travero, a Subsidiary of Alliant Energy Corporation, on its Sale of Logistics Park Dubuque to LOGISTEC
summary: RJM & Company served as the exclusive financial advisor to Travero, a subsidiary of Alliant Energy Corporation, on the sale of its Logistics Park Dubuque (LPD) to LOGISTEC. LPD, a multi-purpose marine terminal on the Upper Mississippi River, will enhance LOGISTEC's inland waterways network, providing strategic routing for agriculture, manufacturing, and energy supply chains. This acquisition strengthens LOGISTEC's Midwest presence and supports LPD's long-term growth by aligning it with an experienced logistics terminal operator.

title: Mississippi cargo terminal sold by Alliant Energy subsidiary
summary: Alliant Energy's subsidiary, Travero, has sold Logistics Park Dubuque (LPD), a 100-acre multimodal marine terminal, to LOGISTEC. RJM & Company advised Travero on the sale. This acquisition expands LOGISTEC's network into the U.S. Midwest and Gulf Coast, enhancing connectivity for agriculture, manufacturing, and energy supply chains.

title: Alliant Energy started at Outperform, citing Iowa's 'unique regulatory construct' (LNT:NASDAQ)
summary: RBC Capital initiated coverage of Alliant Energy (LNT) with an Outperform rating and an $82 price target. The firm cited "embedded conservatism" in the company's management and Iowa's unique regulatory environment as key factors for the positive outlook. This initiation followed the company's Q3 2025 earnings per share compound annual growth rate increase.

title: Alliant Awards Tree Planting Grants to 23 Iowa Communities
summary: Alliant Energy has awarded over $95,000 in grants to 23 Iowa communities through its Community Tree Planting program, part of the One Million Trees initiative. This program, in partnership with Trees Forever, aims to restore tree canopies, boost energy efficiency, and create environmental benefits across Iowa. Since 1990, Alliant Energy and Trees Forever have invested over $8 million and planted more than 1.1 million trees in Iowa and Wisconsin.

title: Royal Bank Of Canada Begins Coverage on Alliant Energy (NASDAQ:LNT)
summary: Royal Bank of Canada has initiated coverage on Alliant Energy (NASDAQ:LNT) with an "Outperform" rating and an $82 price target, indicating a potential 15.43% upside. This positive outlook is in line with the general consensus among analysts, who currently rate the stock as a "Moderate Buy" with an average target of $75.33. Alliant Energy recently surpassed quarterly EPS expectations and saw its revenue increase by 9%.

title: RBC Capital initiates Alliant Energy stock at Outperform on rate base growth
summary: RBC Capital has initiated coverage on Alliant Energy Corp. (NASDAQ:LNT) with an Outperform rating and an $82 price target, citing embedded conservatism in management's EPS growth rate and greater leverage to datacenter announcements. The firm's 2029 EPS estimate is 2% above consensus, driven by accelerated rate base growth. Despite InvestingPro suggesting the stock is overvalued, RBC Capital views the current trading level as an attractive entry point.

title: RBC Capital initiates Alliant Energy stock at Outperform on rate base growth
summary: RBC Capital has initiated coverage on Alliant Energy Corp. (NASDAQ:LNT) with an Outperform rating and an $82 price target, citing embedded conservatism in management's EPS growth rate and stronger leverage to datacenter announcements. The firm also highlighted the positive impact of Iowa's regulatory framework and projects 2029 EPS approximately 2% above consensus, driven by accelerating rate base growth. Despite the stock trading near its 52-week high, RBC Capital considers its valuation attractive compared to peers, while InvestingPro analysis suggests it might be overvalued.

title: BMO raises Alliant Energy Corporation (LNT) target to $78, maintains outperform
summary: BMO Capital Markets has increased its price target for Alliant Energy Corporation (LNT) to $78, up from the previous $76. The firm reiterated an "outperform" rating on the stock, citing the company's strong execution and the positive outlook from its recent analyst day.

title: Linn County supervisors pass first reading of ordinance ahead of proposed Alliant Energy power plant
summary: The Linn County Board of Supervisors has passed the first reading of an ordinance to regulate a proposed Alliant Energy power plant in unincorporated Linn County. The ordinance addresses road use, airport considerations, a waste management plan, and adjusts setback rules allowing for case-by-case approval of smaller setbacks from residential areas. The ordinance requires two more public hearings, and Alliant Energy still needs approval from the Iowa Utilities Commission.

title: Alliant Energy Brings Solar, Wind, and Energy Storage Projects To Iowa
summary: Alliant Energy is expanding its renewable energy infrastructure across Iowa and Wisconsin, focusing on solar, wind, and energy storage projects. The company aims to provide reliable, cost-effective energy to its customers, enhance economic development, and achieve net-zero emissions by 2050. These investments include significant solar and wind farms, battery storage systems, and community partnerships that support local economies and environmental goals.

title: B. Metzler seel. Sohn & Co. AG Sells 38,034 Shares of Alliant Energy Corporation $LNT
summary: B. Metzler seel. Sohn & Co. AG has reduced its stake in Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT) by 45.9%, selling 38,034 shares and retaining 44,879 shares valued at approximately $3.03 million. Other institutional investors like AQR Capital Management and Goldman Sachs Group Inc. have increased their positions. Alliant Energy recently raised its quarterly dividend to $0.535 per share and reported strong Q earnings, beating EPS estimates and growing revenue by 9% year-over-year.

title: BMO Raises Alliant Energy Corporation (LNT) Target to $78, Maintains Outperform
summary: BMO Capital has raised its price target for Alliant Energy Corporation (LNT) to $78, maintaining an Outperform rating following strong Q4 results and a new agreement for the QTS Madison project. Separately, Wells Fargo also increased its price target to $75, citing the company's solid updates, attractive data center growth, and stable regulatory environment. Overall, 7 out of 14 analysts covering LNT recommend a Buy rating, with a 1-year average price target suggesting a 3.73% upside potential.

title: BMO Raises Alliant Energy Corporation (LNT) Target to $78, Maintains Outperform
summary: BMO Capital has raised its price target for Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ: LNT) to $78 from $72, maintaining an Outperform rating after the company's Q4 results. This decision was influenced by a new agreement concerning the QTS Madison project and potential upward revisions to earnings per share. Separately, Wells Fargo also increased its price target for LNT to $75, citing attractive data center growth and the absence of major rate cases during an election period, further reinforcing positive analyst sentiment for the utility company.

title: Alliant Energy Corp. Hits New 52-Week High at $72.65
summary: Alliant Energy Corp. has reached a new 52-week high of $72.65, demonstrating strong performance in the power industry with a 44.03% increase over the past year. The company boasts a market capitalization of $18,280 million, a P/E ratio of 21.00, and an attractive dividend yield of 141.78%. Its financial health is further supported by a debt-to-equity ratio of 1.58 and a return on equity of 11.04%.

title: Alliant Energy outlines 7%+ annual earnings growth target through 2029 as data center demand drives capital plan
summary: Alliant Energy (LNT) presented a new five-year financial outlook, targeting annual earnings per share growth of 7% or more through 2029, driven by increased capital expenditures for data center demand. The company plans to invest $17.6 billion through 2029, with approximately 85% allocated to electric utility projects and the remainder to natural gas. This strategy emphasizes clean energy, grid modernization, and infrastructure to support significant load growth from new data center facilities in Wisconsin and Iowa.

title: Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT) Receives Average Rating of "Moderate Buy" from Brokerages
summary: Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT) has received an average "Moderate Buy" rating from ten brokerage firms, with an average 12-month price target of $74.50. The company recently reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst estimates, and increased its quarterly dividend. Institutional investors have also significantly increased their holdings in Alliant Energy.

title: Alliant weighs Linn County site for Morgan Valley natural gas plant after airport plan dropped
summary: Alliant Energy is considering a new, unincorporated Linn County site for its proposed Morgan Valley Energy Center, a natural gas-fired power plant. This comes after the company deprioritized a previous location near the Eastern Iowa Airport due to community opposition and safety concerns. An informational meeting about the project will be held on Monday, March 23.

title: Allient and Riskgeorge In Compared: Which Tech Stock is the Better Buy?
summary: This article compares Riskgeorge In (OTCMKTS:RSKIA) and Allient (NASDAQ:ALNT), two small-cap computer and technology companies, to determine which is the better investment. While Riskgeorge In offers a higher dividend yield, Allient shows stronger financial performance, greater institutional ownership, and more favorable analyst ratings. The analysis suggests Allient has more upside potential for investors in the small-cap tech sector.

title: Alliant Energy (LNT) director Manu Asthana reports 1,000-share stake
summary: Alliant Energy Corp (LNT) director Manu Asthana has filed an initial ownership report (Form 3) showing a direct holding of 1,000 shares of the company's common stock. This filing indicates current beneficial ownership rather than a recent purchase or sale transaction. The shares are held directly by Mr. Asthana.

title: Alliant Energy enters $400 million term loan agreement with U.S. Bank
summary: Alliant Energy Corp. announced a new $400 million term loan agreement with U.S. Bank, maturing on March 1, 2027. Proceeds from this facility can be used for general corporate purposes, including working capital and refinancing existing debt. The agreement mandates Alliant Energy to maintain a consolidated debt-to-capital ratio not exceeding 65%, and includes specific restrictions on liens and standard default provisions.

title: Alliant Energy Corp enters term loan credit agreement on March 2, 2026 - SEC filing
summary: Alliant Energy Corp entered into a term loan credit agreement on March 2, 2026, as reported in an SEC filing. This news follows several analyst adjustments to Alliant Energy's price target, primarily maintaining outperform ratings. The company recently reported its Q4 2025 earnings, affirming its earnings guidance for 2026, and also increased its quarterly dividend.

title: Alliant Energy Secures New $400 Million Term Loan Facility
summary: Alliant Energy (LNT) has secured a new $400 million term loan facility, with an incremental option for an additional $100 million, maturing in 2027. This facility is intended for general corporate purposes, including working capital and debt refinancing. While it provides liquidity, it also comes with covenants such as a debt-to-capital ratio limit and restrictions on liens, and analysts currently rate LNT as a "Hold" with a $73.00 price target. According to TipRanks’ AI Analyst Spark, LNT is Neutral, citing strong profitability and constructive technical trends despite weak cash generation and rising leverage.

title: Alliant Energy Secures $400 Million Term Loan, Adds $100 Million Incremental Option With U.S. Bank
summary: Alliant Energy has secured a new $400 million term loan, with an additional $100 million incremental option, maturing on March 1, 2027. This agreement, entered with U.S. Bank as Administrative Agent, aims to enhance the company's liquidity for general corporate purposes, including working capital, capital expenditures, and refinancing. The facility includes covenants such as a maximum 65% debt-to-capital ratio and limits on liens, providing significant financial flexibility through the medium term.

title: Alliant Energy enters $400 million term loan agreement with U.S. Bank
summary: Alliant Energy Corp. has secured a $400 million term loan credit agreement with U.S. Bank National Association, set to mature on March 1, 2027. The funds are intended for general corporate purposes, including working capital and debt refinancing, and the agreement includes covenants such as maintaining a consolidated debt-to-capital ratio not exceeding 65%. This follows Alliant Energy's strong Q4 2025 financial results, which saw an EPS of $0.60 and revenue of $1.06 billion, surpassing analyst expectations.

title: Alliant Energy (LNT) secures $400M term loan with 2027 maturity and covenants
summary: Alliant Energy Corporation (LNT) has secured a new $400 million term loan facility, with an option for an additional $100 million, set to mature on March 1, 2027. The utility company can utilize these funds for general corporate purposes, including working capital, capital expenditures, and debt refinancing. The agreement includes a key covenant limiting the consolidated debt-to-capital ratio to not more than 65% and outlines restrictions on placing liens on company assets.

title: LNT Financials: Income Statement, Balance Sheet & Cash Flow
summary: This article provides a detailed financial overview of Alliant Energy Corp (LNT), presenting its income statement, balance sheet, cash flow statement, and key financial ratios through fiscal year 2025. It highlights the company's profitability, growth, leverage, liquidity, and returns, noting its moderate financial health with some concerns regarding liquidity and financial distress based on the Altman Z-Score. The report also addresses frequently asked questions about LNT's financial performance.

title: OGE Energy (NYSE:OGE) - Stock Analysis
summary: This report provides a comprehensive stock analysis of OGE Energy (NYSE:OGE), highlighting its current market position, financial health, and future growth prospects. Key takeaways include sustained customer growth, strategic infrastructure investments, and a stable financial position supporting long-term profitability. The article also covers valuation, past performance, dividends, and recent news updates for the company.

title: In New Corporate Responsibility Report, Alliant Energy Announces Progress Toward Clean Energy Vision
summary: Alliant Energy released its 2021 Corporate Responsibility Report, detailing significant progress in renewable energy and its Clean Energy Vision. The report highlights a 42% CO2 reduction since 2005, the retirement of 1,100 MW of coal, and plans to plant over one million trees. Alliant Energy is committed to eliminating coal by 2040 and achieving net-zero carbon emissions by 2050, alongside strong social and governance initiatives.

title: Alliant Energy Corp - Enters Term Loan Credit Agreement On March 2, 2026 - SEC Filing
summary: Alliant Energy Corp (NASDAQ:LNT) announced that it has entered into a term loan credit agreement on March 2, 2026. This information was disclosed in a recent SEC filing. The news highlights a significant corporate finance development for Alliant Energy.

title: Mitsubishi UFJ Asset Management Co. Ltd. Has $35.63 Million Position in Alliant Energy Corporation $LNT
summary: Mitsubishi UFJ Asset Management Co. Ltd. increased its stake in Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT) by 9.1% in the third quarter, bringing its total holdings to 528,539 shares valued at $35.63 million. Other institutional investors like Carnegie Investment Counsel and Liberty One Investment Management LLC also adjusted their positions in the company. Alliant Energy recently announced a quarterly dividend increase and reported earnings that beat analyst estimates.

title: Bahl & Gaynor Inc. Acquires 74,086 Shares of Alliant Energy Corporation $LNT
summary: Bahl & Gaynor Inc. significantly increased its stake in Alliant Energy Corporation, boosting its holdings by 12.3% and now owning 675,388 shares worth $45.5 million. This increase is part of a broader trend of institutional investor activity in Alliant Energy, which also recently reported strong quarterly earnings, topping analyst estimates, and declared an increased quarterly dividend. The company maintains an average analyst rating of "Moderate Buy" with a consensus target price of $74.50.

title: Interstate Power and Light Company Prices Debt Offering
summary: Interstate Power and Light Company (IPL), a subsidiary of Alliant Energy Corporation, announced the pricing of a public offering of $300 million in 5.600% senior debentures due in 2055. The proceeds will be used to reduce outstanding capital under its receivables program, outstanding commercial paper, and/or for general corporate purposes. The offering is expected to close on September 11, 2025, and was marketed through a group of underwriters including KeyBanc Capital Markets Inc. and PNC Capital Markets LLC.

title: Alliant Energy Celebrates First Community Solar Garden in Company History
summary: Alliant Energy celebrated its first community solar project in Fond du Lac, Wisconsin, a 1-megawatt solar garden capable of powering 1,000 homes. This initiative allows customers to benefit from solar power without personal installation, contributing to Alliant Energy's Clean Energy Blueprint and goal of net-zero carbon dioxide emissions by 2050. Almost 95% of the available solar blocks have been subscribed, highlighting the community's interest in renewable energy.

title: Construction in Motion on Alliant Energy's Solar in Wisconsin
summary: Alliant Energy has begun construction on its initial solar projects in Wisconsin after receiving approval from the Public Service Commission of Wisconsin. These projects, part of the company's Clean Energy Blueprint, aim to accelerate the transition to renewable energy, reduce carbon emissions, and provide economic benefits to local communities through job creation and revenue. Once all 12 proposed projects are operational, Alliant Energy will become the largest owner and operator of solar energy in Wisconsin, providing clean energy to nearly 300,000 homes.

title: Alliant Energy (LNT) CEO receives RSU grants and withholds shares for taxes
summary: Alliant Energy's President and CEO, Lisa M. Barton, reported receiving Restricted Stock Unit (RSU) grants on February 19, 2026, totaling 77,060 shares. Concurrently, she disposed of 28,594 shares at $70.01 each to cover tax obligations related to these grants. The RSUs convert to common stock on a one-to-one basis and are scheduled to vest on December 31, 2028.

title: Assessing Alliant Energy (LNT) Valuation After Reaffirmed 2026 Guidance And Analyst Optimism
summary: Alliant Energy (LNT) is gaining investor attention after strong financial performance in Q4 2025, reaffirmed 2026 guidance, and progress on major customer projects. While Simply Wall St's narrative suggests a 2.4% undervaluation with a fair value of $74.09, their Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model indicates the stock is overvalued at $72.34. The company's valuation hinges on sustained customer demand, decarbonization efforts, infrastructure modernization, and favorable regulation.

title: Alliant Energy Stock: Quiet Utility, Big Yield – Is It Enough Now?
summary: Alliant Energy Corp. (NASDAQ: LNT) is presented as a stable, income-generating utility focused on Iowa and Wisconsin, offering modest growth and a dividend, positioning it between bond-like stability and growth. The article examines how rising interest rates, grid investments, and regulatory risks are reshaping its risk-reward profile, detailing what investors, analysts, and social trading communities should consider given these factors. It concludes that while suitable for income-focused and defensive portfolios, it won't meet aggressive capital gains expectations.

title: EVRG Financials: Income Statement, Balance Sheet & Cash Flow
summary: This article provides a comprehensive overview of Evergy Inc.'s (EVRG) financial statements for fiscal year 2025, including income statements, balance sheets, and cash flow data, derived from SEC filings. It details key financial metrics such as revenue, net income, EPS, and discusses financial health signals like profitability, growth, leverage, liquidity, cash flow, and returns, noting areas of strength and potential concern. The report also includes an Altman Z-Score, Piotroski F-Score, and analysis of earnings quality and interest coverage to assess the company's financial stability and performance.
